com.testingtech.tworkbench.ttman.server.api

Interface ICampaignExecution

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IExecutionBase
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    RemoteExecutionClient

    public interface ICampaignExecution
extends IExecutionBase
    Represents a job queue for executing test campaigns. All execution jobs will be submitted to a single queue shared by all clients.

    • Field Summary

      • Fields inherited from interface com.testingtech.tworkbench.ttman.server.api.IExecutionBase

        AVOID_NAME_CLASH, OVERRIDE, SYNC

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      ExecuteCampaignJob enqueueCamapign(java.lang.String projectName, java.lang.String clfPath, ExecutorRequirements executorRequirements)
      Enqueues a campaign execution.

      • Methods inherited from interface com.testingtech.tworkbench.ttman.server.api.IExecutionBase

        connect, deleteProject, disconnect, enableLogging, getAttributes, getProjectManager, killTestSystem, listExecutionJobs, publishProject, reloadTestSystem, setEnvironmentVariables, setJVMArguments, setJVMArguments, setModuleParameters, setTestAdapterConfiguration, shutdownServer, stopExecution, stopExecution

    • Method Detail

      • enqueueCamapign

        ExecuteCampaignJob enqueueCamapign(java.lang.String projectName,
                                   java.lang.String clfPath,
                                   ExecutorRequirements executorRequirements)
                            throws java.io.IOException,
                                   AuthenticationException
        Enqueues a campaign execution.
        Parameters:
        projectName - project name in the server workspace
        clfPath - project relative campaign file path
        executorRequirements - requirements for campaign execution
        Returns:
        an execution job that may be used to cancel and follow the execution request
        Throws:
        java.io.IOException
        AuthenticationException
